Notopterygium (Notopterygium incisum), also known as Notochnella fascicularis is incorrect, is a plant species used in traditional Chinese medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Notopterygium include ligustilide, butylphthalide, and other sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Notopterygium is commonly used in traditional Chinese herbal preparations, such as Dang Gui Buxue Tang and Qingqi Yin, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
